shortcuts.description: Current state of edtech

shortcuts.name: Online Learning Snapshot
shortcuts.prompt: What's the current state of online learning? Cover the major platforms, MOOC and bootcamp dynamics, and degree vs. certificate value.
shortcuts.description: Current state of online learning

shortcuts.name: K-12 Edtech Watch
shortcuts.prompt: What's happening in K-12 edtech? Cover district adoption trends, the leading platforms across categories, pandemic-era platform fates, and AI's role in classrooms.
shortcuts.description: Signals worth tracking in k-12 edtech

shortcuts.name: Skills-Based Learning
shortcuts.prompt: What's happening in skills-based learning and alternative credentials? Cover bootcamps, certificate programs, employer-led training, and how employers are evaluating these credentials.
shortcuts.description: Skills-based learning and alternative credentials

shortcuts.name: Best Tools to Learn A Skill
shortcuts.prompt: What are the best tools for learning [language, math, coding, professional skills, etc.]? Cover the leading platforms and apps, what each does best, and how they compare on price and approach.
shortcuts.description: Top picks worth knowing about right now

shortcuts.name: Ideas in the Air
shortcuts.prompt: What are the most discussed ideas and intellectual conversations happening right now? Cover essays, books, and debates that are getting traction across the cultural and intellectual conversation.
shortcuts.description: Conversations and concepts gaining traction

shortcuts.name: Ideas Worth Knowing
shortcuts.prompt: What recently published books or essays are introducing ideas worth knowing about? Cover work that's being widely discussed and likely to shape conversation going forward.
shortcuts.description: Recent books and essays making real waves

shortcuts.name: Opinion Across the Spectrum
shortcuts.prompt: What are the most-discussed opinion and commentary pieces from across the political and intellectual spectrum this week? Note what each is arguing and what's driving the conversation.
shortcuts.description: Notable commentary from across the spectrum

shortcuts.name: Best Long Reads
shortcuts.prompt: What are the best recent long-form essays and articles worth reading right now? Cover pieces from major publications and independent writers across topics — politics, ideas, culture, history, science.
shortcuts.description: Top picks worth knowing about right now

shortcuts.name: Applied Ethics Watch
shortcuts.prompt: What are the most active debates in applied ethics right now (AI ethics, bioethics, business ethics, environmental ethics, etc.)? For each: the central question, the main positions, and recent developments.
shortcuts.description: Signals worth tracking in applied ethics

shortcuts.name: Moral Philosophy Compared
shortcuts.prompt: Compare the major moral philosophical frameworks on their core claims, strengths, and limitations. Note where they tend to agree and disagree on common cases.
shortcuts.description: How the major moral frameworks actually differ

shortcuts.name: Help With an Ethical Dilemma
shortcuts.prompt: Help me think through an ethical dilemma I'm facing. Ask me about the situation, the considerations on different sides, and what I value — then help me reason through it carefully without telling me what to do.
shortcuts.description: Help me think through an ethical dilemma I'm facing

shortcuts.name: Philosophy Primer
shortcuts.prompt: Give me a primer on [philosophical topic, school of thought, or thinker]. Cover the core ideas, the historical context, key arguments and critiques, and recommended reading to go deeper.
shortcuts.description: Background and basics for philosophy

shortcuts.name: Philosophy Reading Recommendations
shortcuts.prompt: Recommend philosophy reading based on what I'm interested in. Ask about my current familiarity, the questions or topics that interest me, and how technical I want it — then suggest a thoughtful sequence.
shortcuts.description: Recommend philosophy reading based on what I'm interested in

shortcuts.name: Underrated Moments in History
shortcuts.prompt: What are some lesser-known but consequential moments in history that deserve more attention? Cover events, decisions, or figures whose importance often gets missed in standard narratives.
shortcuts.description: How things got to where they are now

shortcuts.name: Historical Topic Deep Dive
shortcuts.prompt: Give me a deep introduction to [historical topic, era, or event]. Cover the essential context, key figures, major events, what changed because of it, and the dominant historical interpretations.
shortcuts.description: A deeper read of context, dynamics, and what matters

shortcuts.name: Historical Parallels
shortcuts.prompt: What historical parallels are most relevant to [current event or trend]? Cover the parallels carefully — what's similar, what's different, and what we can and can't reasonably take from them.
shortcuts.description: Parallels from history worth weighing carefully

shortcuts.name: Recommended History Reading
shortcuts.prompt: Recommend history books on [topic, era, or region]. Ask about my prior familiarity and how academic vs. popular I want — then suggest a thoughtful reading list with brief justifications.
shortcuts.description: Books worth reading on the subject

shortcuts.name: Historiography Update
shortcuts.prompt: How has the historical understanding of [topic or period] changed in recent decades? Cover what new sources, methods, or interpretive shifts have changed the picture.
shortcuts.description: Latest movement on historiography

shortcuts.name: History of a Topic
shortcuts.prompt: Walk me through the history of [topic — a country, idea, technology, institution, etc.]. Cover the origins, key turning points, current state, and how to understand its trajectory.
shortcuts.description: How things got to where they are now

shortcuts.name: Religion News Snapshot
shortcuts.prompt: Give me a snapshot of significant recent developments in major world religions and the religious landscape globally. Cover institutional news, theological debates, and intersections with politics and culture.
shortcuts.description: Current state of religion news

shortcuts.name: Vatican and Catholic Church Watch
shortcuts.prompt: What's happening in the Catholic Church and at the Vatican right now? Cover papal activity, major appointments, doctrinal debates, and the dominant stories across the global Church.
shortcuts.description: Signals worth tracking in vatican and catholic church

shortcuts.name: US Religious Landscape
shortcuts.prompt: What's happening with religion in the US right now? Cover trends in affiliation and practice across traditions, religion-and-politics dynamics, and major institutional stories.
shortcuts.description: The state of play across US religious

shortcuts.name: Religion and Politics
shortcuts.prompt: What's happening at the intersection of religion and politics right now? Cover key issues being debated, the political activity of religious groups, and how religious identity is shaping politics across countries.
shortcuts.description: Where religion is shaping political debate

shortcuts.name: Spirituality and Meaning Trends
shortcuts.prompt: What's happening in non-institutional spirituality and meaning-making? Cover trends in mindfulness, secular ritual, "spiritual but not religious" identities, and the cultural conversations around meaning.
shortcuts.description: Non-institutional spirituality and meaning-making

shortcuts.name: Religious Tradition Primer
shortcuts.prompt: Give me a primer on [religion or tradition]. Cover core beliefs, major branches and their differences, key practices, the historical development, and how it's lived in the world today.
shortcuts.description: Background and basics for religious tradition

shortcuts.name: Compare Religious Traditions
shortcuts.prompt: Compare [traditions or beliefs] on [a specific question or topic]. Cover where they agree, where they differ, and how each tradition has approached the question over time.
shortcuts.description: Side-by-side on a contested question

shortcuts.name: Timeline of Key Dates
shortcuts.prompt: Give me a timeline of the key dates and milestones in the topic of: {TOPIC}. Cover the most consequential events that shaped this space — when each happened, what it was, and why it mattered. Include both the historical foundations and recent inflection points. Organize chronologically.
shortcuts.description: Key moments that shaped this space

shortcuts.name: Latest Research and Reports
shortcuts.prompt: What's the latest serious research, reports, and studies on the topic of: {TOPIC}? Cover peer-reviewed papers, industry reports, government findings, and credible analysis published recently. For each: what it found, who published it, and why it matters. Skip blog posts and press releases.
shortcuts.description: Recent serious studies and analysis

shortcuts.name: What Just Changed
shortcuts.prompt: What has meaningfully changed in {topic} recently — the development that, if I'd been away, I'd most need to know? Explain what shifted, what it replaced, and how it changes the way to think about this topic going forward.
shortcuts.description: The recent shift that resets the picture

shortcuts.name: The Cutting Edge
shortcuts.prompt: Where is the frontier in {topic} right now — the questions being actively worked on, the new approaches being tried, or the discoveries being made? Explain what's driving progress at the edge and what might come of it.
shortcuts.description: Where the active work is happening now

shortcuts.name: Recent Milestones
shortcuts.prompt: What are the most notable recent milestones in {topic} — discoveries, achievements, releases, or events worth knowing about? For each, explain what happened and why it mattered, and note which will still matter a year from now.
shortcuts.description: The notable moments of the last while

shortcuts.name: The Big Ideas
shortcuts.prompt: What are the handful of core ideas at the heart of {topic} — the concepts everything else builds on? Explain each clearly and show how they connect, so I come away understanding the foundation rather than a pile of facts.
shortcuts.description: The core concepts everything rests on

shortcuts.name: How It Actually Works
shortcuts.prompt: Walk me through how {topic} actually works — the real mechanics, steps, or moving parts, not just the surface description. Use a concrete example to make it tangible, and point out where people's intuitions usually go wrong.
shortcuts.description: The mechanics under the hood

shortcuts.name: The Main Types
shortcuts.prompt: Break down the main types, categories, or branches within {topic}. Explain what distinguishes each one, how they relate, and when each matters — so I can tell them apart and place anything new I encounter.
shortcuts.description: The varieties, kinds, or schools within it

shortcuts.name: How the Pieces Fit Together
shortcuts.prompt: Give me a map of {topic} — the main parts, ideas, or players and how they relate to each other. Help me see the overall structure so I know where any given detail fits, and point out the connections people most often miss.
shortcuts.description: A map of the whole landscape

shortcuts.name: Notable Examples
shortcuts.prompt: Show me the most illuminating examples of {topic} — the specific cases, instances, or stories that make the idea concrete. For each, explain what it demonstrates and why it's a good example to remember.
shortcuts.description: The concrete cases that make it click

shortcuts.name: How It Compares
shortcuts.prompt: Help me place {topic} by comparing it to the things it's most often confused with or grouped alongside. Explain the key differences and similarities, so I understand not just what it is, but what it isn't.
shortcuts.description: Where it sits among similar things

shortcuts.name: Frequently Asked Questions
shortcuts.prompt: Answer the most common questions people have about {topic} — the things a curious newcomer would naturally ask. Give a clear, direct answer to each, and prioritize the questions whose answers are surprising or commonly gotten wrong.
shortcuts.description: The questions everyone asks

shortcuts.name: Cheat Sheet
shortcuts.prompt: Build me a quick-reference cheat sheet for {topic} — the key terms, facts, distinctions, and rules of thumb worth keeping at a glance. Keep it tight and scannable, and prioritize what I'd actually want to look up again.
shortcuts.description: The quick reference to keep handy

shortcuts.name: Best Resources to Go Deeper
shortcuts.prompt: Point me to the best resources for genuinely learning {topic} — standout books, courses, documentaries, sites, or guides. For each, say who it's best for and what it does better than the alternatives, and suggest a sensible order to take them in.
shortcuts.description: Where to learn this properly

shortcuts.name: Why It Matters
shortcuts.prompt: Make the case for why {topic} matters. Explain its significance — what it affects, what depends on it, and what we'd lose or misunderstand without it — and distinguish the reasons it genuinely matters from the reasons it's merely talked about.
shortcuts.description: The significance, plainly stated

shortcuts.name: The Big Picture
shortcuts.prompt: Zoom out and place {topic} in its broader context. How does it connect to other fields, events, or ideas, and what larger story is it part of? Help me see why it doesn't exist in isolation.
shortcuts.description: How it connects to the wider world

shortcuts.name: What Drives It
shortcuts.prompt: What are the underlying forces that shape {topic} — the causes, drivers, or conditions that make it what it is? Trace cause and effect, and separate the factors that genuinely drive things from the ones that just come along for the ride.
shortcuts.description: The causes and forces underneath

shortcuts.name: What's Surprising
shortcuts.prompt: What's surprising or counterintuitive about {topic} — the things that run against common assumptions or that even interested people often get wrong? For each, explain what people expect, what's actually true, and why the gap exists.
shortcuts.description: The counterintuitive parts

shortcuts.name: What We Don't Know Yet
shortcuts.prompt: What are the real open questions in {topic} — the things even informed people genuinely can't answer yet? Separate "unknown because it's unsettled" from "unknown because no one's looked," and note which answers would change the most if we got them.
shortcuts.description: The genuine open questions

shortcuts.name: Strengths and Limitations
shortcuts.prompt: Give me an honest assessment of {topic}'s strengths and limitations — where it works well, where it breaks down, and the tradeoffs involved. Avoid both boosterism and cynicism, and tell me what to keep in mind before relying on it.
shortcuts.description: What it does well and where it falls short

shortcuts.name: Overhyped vs. Underrated
shortcuts.prompt: When it comes to {topic}, what gets more attention than it deserves, and what's quietly more important than people realize? Explain the gap between perception and reality for each, and what's driving the mismatch.
shortcuts.description: Where attention is mispriced
